The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise determine if the unexpended balances accrue to the National Government or to the Local Government Unit in case of counterpart funds. If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, the Local Government Unit shall take the following actions: a) For funds accruing to the National Government Agencies, (1) revisit or review the MOA particularly on the agreed disposition of unused balances; (2) confer and reconcile the account with the source agency to ensure the correct balance of the account; (3) once reconciled, return immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the source agency or to the Bureau of Treasury, as the case may be; and b) For counterpart funds, the Local Finance Committee shall conduct the necessary evaluation and thereafter give recommendations on the proper disposition of the funds in relation to the priority projects of the Municipality.

We recommended: a) Ensure orderliness in the planning and budget processes, as demonstrated by compliance with the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for LGUs; b) Prepare the MDRRMFIP in accordance with the form specified in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002; c) Ensure that all DRRMF-funded projects and activities are included in the MDRRMFIP, even those activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the STF; d) Take concrete actions to address the issues on delayed or non-implementation of funded PPAs, taking into consideration the new normal brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, thereby ensuring resiliency despite the current challenges; and e) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entry for the recording of the unexpended portion of DRRMF, and carefully prepare and review financial reports to enhance its reliability and accuracy.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
on the following courses of action to be taken by the agency head, viz:
a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted
by the municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding, and reporting of its
PPE:
b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual
physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required
standards and procedures in planning, execution, and reconciliation of GSO and
Accounting records;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards
and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular
reconciliation of records and conduct further investigation into the circumstances
surrounding the loss of some items that were not found during the actual count. If
still unlocated, determine the concerned personnel liable for the loss and require
them to pay for the cost of the property since the required request for
accountability has not been filed within the prescribed period;
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosure of relevant information in
the Notes to Financial Statements for the subsequent years for appropriate
understanding;
e) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts all
completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is applicable and
direct the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the
Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the
accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular
No. 2015-008 and submit the pertinent reports as prescribed by existing laws,
rules and regulations; and
f) Give priority to the directives set under the COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts which will provide benefits to the municipality.

to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) reclassify the current portion of Loans Payable–Domestic for better presentation
of liabilities of the LGU in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-

b) make reconciliation of the accounts with the lending institution for the
Municipality’s financial liabilities and effect valid reconciling items in the books of
accounts;
c) provide disclosure of relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements for
the subsequent years for appropriate understanding.

a) For funds accruing to the National Government Agencies, (1) revisit or review the

b) Enforce strictly the provision of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and require the concerned
officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to immediately settle the
same; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the aforementioned
accountable officers and provide a copy of the demand letters to the Audit Team as
confirmation of the Municipal Accountant's action to demand liquidation/settlement.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Maximize its authority and power to collect taxes, fees and other charges;
b) Tap additional sources of income as authorized under RA 7160 to increase
revenues and avoid total dependence on the internal revenue allotments; and
c) Strictly implement the tax ordinances enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan relative
to revenue generation program.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed with
the following courses of actions:
a) Ensure that the 20% development fund be optimally utilized for socio-economic
and environmental outcomes pursuant to the intent of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 by prioritizing the
projects, programs and activities which are attainable/implementation-ready to
ensure efficient and effective fund governance;
b) Reinforce and/or adjust existing planning and budgeting practices and techniques
to further improve the orderliness of the Municipality’s processes to be able to
comply with the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for LGUs; and
c) Formulate realistic and attainable work plan by installing performance tracking
mechanisms to monitor the implementation of existing projects and taking into
consideration the factors affecting performance identified in the monitoring
process.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Ensure orderliness in the planning and budget processes, as demonstrated by
compliance with the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for LGUs;
b) Prepare the MDRRMFIP in accordance with the form specified in Annex A of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;
c) Ensure that all the DRRMF-funded projects and activities are included in the
MDRRMFIP, even those activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and
DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the STF;
d) Take concrete actions to address the issues on delayed or non-implementation of
funded PPAs, taking into consideration the new normal brought about by the
COVID-19 pandemic, thereby ensuring resiliency despite the current challenges; and
e) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entry for the
under recording of the unexpended portion of DRRMF, and carefully prepare and
review financial reports to enhance its reliability and accuracy.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
with the following courses of actions:
a) Promote media literacy and improve information dissemination to assist
constituents in identifying fake news through raising awareness of COVID-19 and
the importance of vaccination by supporting reliable and responsible journalism,
esspecially in social media and engaging respectful dialogue with the public.
b) To address the shortage of vaccination personnel, the LGU shall mobilize
volunteers and non-healthcare workers to assist with vaccination efforts, expand
training programs to increase the pool of qualified personnel, and offer incentives
to encourage participation in the vaccination program.
c) Collaborate with Barangay Officials who have influence in areas with a high
number of unvaccinated individuals, such as community leaders, tribal leaders, or
religious leaders through engaging in a dialogue to understand their concerns
and challenges and obtain their support in promoting vaccination.
d) Develop a contingency plan to ensure that vaccinations can continue even if there
are peace and order disruptions such as taking advantage of technology to reach
and educate target beneficiaries.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
to:
a) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for
the timely submission of the said reports to the COA audit team;
b) Require the Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD
projects, activities and programs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD
objectives;
c) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were
provided; and
d) Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with
adequate, updated and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will
provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy
formulation and establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system to come up
with a more GAD responsive and related programs and activities.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
to:
a) Strictly adhere to the provision set forth in the DILG MC No. 2019-149 especially
on the prompt and updated posting of the required documents in at least three (3)
conspicuous places;
b) Require the FDP Focal Person to be prompt and accurate on the posting of the
required documents and instruct the Department Heads of concerned offices for
the timely submission of the reports; and
c) Maximize the utilization of the FDP Board and website of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
h. Require the General Services Officer to maintain updated Property
Cards per category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded
in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records,
and thereafter as the transaction occurs;
i. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards
consistent with their accounting records;
j. Require both offices to reconcile property records ensuring that the
balances shown in the prepared RPCPPE agreed with the supply
records and the book balances in the accounting records, and vice
versa;
k. For any resulting difference, the Accounting and the General Services
Office should work out on tracing the reconciling items, establish its
existence/nonexistence and determine from there any further action
that may be undertaken;
l. Maintain and renew all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment
every three years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in
the prepared RPCPPE.
m. Ensure that no PPE are distributed to its end-user without ARE and
property tags with complete information such as, tag inventory
number, property number, serial number of the property, description
and other salient information.
n. Prepare and submit reconciled copy of the RPCPPE to the COA Auditors
on or before the 30th day of the ensuing year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and record the
depreciation of the abovementioned PPE accounts by a debit to depreciation
expense or debit to prior years‟ adjustments for the current year‟s depreciation and
prior years‟ depreciation, respectively and a credit to accumulated depreciation in
accordance with the guidelines set by relevant COA regulations in the computation
of depreciation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect
adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on
the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should maintain a separate
book of accounts solely for the Philhealth Capitation Fund for the receipt and
utilization of the same to facilitate timely preparation, submission and evaluation of
the required reports.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should maintain special
accounts for market and 20% development fund operations in accordance with
the provisions of Chapter 6, Volume I of NGAS so that management would be
informed on the financial position and condition of each special account as of a
given time to guide them in taking significant actions to boost the operation of
each account.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management maximize the services of three (3)
present revenue collection officers and stop the practice of assigning the tasks of
collecting to personnel hired as job orders.
Hire additional personnel for the office of the Municipal Treasurer when
necessary.

Recommendation:
We recommended the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and
prioritize the construction of sanitary landfill as final disposal site for solid wastes.
This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the
guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal‟s Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be
approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the
Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the General
Services Officer/Supply Officer to furnish COA copies of perfected
contracts/purchase orders and inspection reports within the prescribed period for
timely review, evaluation and inspection.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management discontinue the practice of allowing
deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending
institutions from the personnel's payroll other than those mandated by law. These
personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly
remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors.
Ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of at least P5,000.00
for CY 2018 pursuant to the General Appropriations Act of 2018.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Members of the Municipal School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirements set forth in Section 7 (b) of the
Republic Act No. 10410 by allocating in Local School Board Budget
adequate funds for various activities in support of the implementation of
the ECCD Program, and comply with the guidelines on allowable charges
against the Special Education Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint
Circular No. 1. S.2017 of DepEd, DBM, and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council,
Child Development Teachers, Day Care Workers, and other stakeholders
of the ECCD System in planning and budget preparation for the
implementation of the ECCD Programs.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer/GSO adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 51 of the MNGAS for
LGUs on the proper recording and reporting of transactions which requires that all
regular purchases of supplies should be booked through the inventory account
whether it is consumed or not within the accounting period and maintenance of
subsidiary ledger cards. The Municipal Treasurer or each Department Head should
conduct an inventory to establish the existence of the office supplies, medical, dental,
and laboratory supplies and drugs, agricultural supplies, medicines and other items/
inventories for stock to serve as basis of recording the same in the books of accounts.
They should also maintain stock cards for control and monitoring purposes and
submit the Summary of Supplies and Material Issued, supported with the Requisition
and Issuance Slips to the Municipal Accountant, which shall serve as basis for
recording the issuance of supplies and materials and the preparation of the Journal
Entry Voucher.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the management to:
a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under
Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.
b. Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or
satisfactory explanation is received within thirty (30) days, cause the
withholding of salary due to the accountable officer/s.
c. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable
officers who have previous unsettled cash advances.
d. Institute appropriate administrative and/or legal actions against the
accountable officers for non-compliance with the above-cited
regulations, if warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management discontinue granting cash advances to
elective officials for purposes other than stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-
002, dated February 10,1997 and Section 339 of RA 7160.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management should strictly administer and
supervise the implementation of development projects and likewise monitor its
implementation. Also, management should evaluate the status of the unimplemented
projects to determine whether these are still feasible for implementation. In the
event that such projects are identified to be no longer achievable, management shall
make amendments in the Annual Investment Plan and propose other viable
development projects/programs to be funded out of the 20% Development and local
fund.

We recommended that the management should implement effective control system on the property management through the following: a. The Local Chief Executive shall require the periodic physical count of property annually and the reconciliation between the accounting and property records after inventory taking. A report thereat shall be made using the report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be submitted to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the ensuing year; and b. The designated GSO shall prepare and issue Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) which shall be renewed every three (3) years; and also maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with the accounting records.
